Reaction No. 35374
0.5<O2(g)> + Sb(s) - e-1 = SbO+1

Note: SbO+1 is not considered to exist in aqueous solution. It would be hydrated. The
predominant equivalent species is Sb+3_OH-1(4). However, SbO+1 is left here because
it appears elsewhere. This should be OK as long as this (single) reference
equilibrium operates everywhere the same. All w 0, otherwise interferes with
calculations

Reaction No. 54982
2<O2(g)> + S(s) + 2<e-1> = SO4-2

Note: From Wolery & Colon [17Woc_29520], p. 654 footnote to Table 5: "USGS 1259 gives
the Gibbs energy of formation of SO4-2 as 117.970 kcal/mol. This is a typographical
error. The intended value (from the cited source, NBS 270-1) was 177.97 kcal/mol.
This becomes 744.529 kJ/mol at 1 bar." This is a critical reaction. Knock-on effects
of the older values are being slowly superseded. However, they cannot just be
abandoned too quickly because this produces bad inconsistencies elsewhere, esp. re
BaSO4(s), which corrupt S(s)/SO4-2 equilibria more problematically. I guess things
will eventually end up closer to lgK0 = 130.0

Reaction No. 58261
Al(s) + 1.5<O2(g)> + H2(g) + e-1 = AlO(OH)2-1

Note: The species AlO2-, corresponding to AlO(OH)2-1, was proposed by Russell et al.,
[55RET_18518]. It has also appeared on different occasions in the Russian literature.
We found no sign of such under 'normal' conditions. I doubt it ever exists but the
matter awaits definitive resolution when Al solubilities at higher temperatures and
(very high Na concs.) are fully understood. PMM (31/5/2013)

Reaction No. 73424

Note: This reaction is problematic. There are issues with the principal literature
source, which we currently assume are typographical (relating to the sign only being
explicit at top of each Table). There are also issues with the concentrated KOH
medium - various compilations have taken lgK0 from lgK without compunction. lgK0 = -
15. by collective decision (MF & PM) 22/7/20. Was -11.8 [EDH] lgK0 = -16 by PMM to
match Roper et al. re set of MSb2O6(s) 23/7/20

                                   Data Assessment


                                       – Page 761 of 785 –
JESS Thermodynamic Database v8.9                                e-1



Weight                       Assessment
9        highest accuracy
8        multi-determined; excellent dependability
7        trustworthy; outstanding single determination
6        better than average reliability
5        average single determination
4        some doubt
3        poorly known
2        guess to achieve consistency (or just as bad)
1        no information but consistent
0        problematic/inconsistent




                                          Techniques


Abbrev                        Method
CDF      Definition
CMN      Several experimental values (crit.)
CRV      Critical review
EAE      Automated extrapolation
EBP      By Proxy
EDH      Debye-Hueckel corrections
EES      Personal estimate
EFE      Free Energy relations
EIU      Critical IUPAC review (estim.)
ELC      Linear Combination of Reactions
EMU      Method unknown
ENS      Not specified
EOD      Calculated from data of others
EOR      Other Reaction Constants
EPZ      Pitzer model correction
ERG      Relative Gibbs energy
ERP      Regression procedure
ESC      Standard conditions anchor

JESS Thermodynamic Database v8.9                                         e-1

MAG    Silver electrode e.m.f.
MAN    Chemical analysis
MCL    Calorimetry
MCU    Copper electrode e.m.f.
MCV    Cyclic voltammetry
MDG    Combination of Thermodynamic Data
MDM    Emf by differential method (buffer capacity)
MEF    Electromotoric force, not specified
MGL    Glass electrode
MHG    Emf with amalgam electrode
MHY    Emf with hydrogen electrode
MIS    Ion selective electrode
MPH    pH method, not specified
MPL    Polarography

MTD    Temperature difference
